problem: Decider prompt at 577 lines — 131% over the 250-line ceiling. Grew 131 lines since last report (was 446). 5th consecutive report flagging this. The prompt contains ~250 lines of reference/procedural material that is highly mechanical: patch JSON schema (~30 lines at step 6), severity matrix (~20 lines), self-apply procedure with full bash scripts (~100 lines at step 6b), expansion integration procedure (~60 lines at step 2a within step 2), and acknowledged failures processing (~35 lines at step 1e).

proposed_fix.summary: Extract four blocks into reference files: (1) Self-apply procedure (step 6b, ~100 lines of bash scripts and error handling) into analyst-selfapply.md, (2) Patch schema + severity matrix (~50 lines) into decider-patch-schema.md, (3) Expansion integration procedure (within step 2, ~60 lines) into decider-expansion-integration.md, (4) Acknowledged failures processing (step 1e, ~35 lines) into decider-failures.md. Main prompt shrinks from 577 to ~330 lines. Each reference file is read at the relevant step.

### 6. Generate Suggested Patches

**Read `monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patch-schema.md` for patch JSON format, severity definitions, and field targeting rules.**
files_to_modify.explanation: Replace ~50 lines of schema/severity definitions with a reference read.

tradeoffs: GAIN: ~245 fewer lines in main prompt, saving ~1,200 tokens per run across ~72 daily decider runs (20min schedule) = ~86,000 tokens/day. Decider only loads reference material when the relevant step has work. RISK: Agent must do 1-3 extra file reads per run, adding ~3-5 seconds. The self-apply procedure is complex bash — if the reference file is not read, the decider would skip self-applying. Mitigated by making the read instruction mandatory and prominent. SERENDIPITY LOSS: Low. The bash scripts and JSON schemas are mechanical — having them in context during triage steps provides no analytical benefit.
estimated_impact: ~245 lines removed from main prompt. ~86,000 tokens/day saved at current run frequency. No quality impact on triage or patch quality.
why_human_needed: Human needs to: (1) identify exact line ranges for extraction, (2) verify the replacement read instructions are clear enough that the decider won't skip them, (3) test that the decider still successfully self-applies after the refactor. Suggest running 2-3 decider cycles in parallel (old prompt vs new prompt) to verify no regression.
actual_result: decider.md 577→453 lines (-124). Patch schema + self-apply extracted to 1 reference file.
